title: Hattie's Rainy Day Birthday Adventure
summary: On a rainy morning, Hattie the Mouse and her friend Mischief set out on a rainy adventure after a breakfast of bacon and pancakes. Clad in raincoats and galoshes, they navigate puddles and mud, befriending Wesley the Worm and a helpful digger named Whopper along the way. Whopper helps smooth a road for them, and they continue to a shop on Cornell Street to buy a sparkly orange purse for Mistress Mouse. The journey ends with a joyous third birthday celebration for Mistress Mouse, complete with chocolate cake and the presentation of her perfect gift.

highlightQuote.text: Now there will be no stars. Now there's some stars. Now there's some more. Now there's a lot of stars.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now there will be no stars. Now there's some stars. Now there's some more. Now there's a lot of stars.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's tell a Hattie story. Always about Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what's our story about Hattie going to be about tonight?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You just keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, you just need to tell the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You're right, I was just about to start telling it. Once upon a time, Hattie the mouse had just finished her delicious dinner. What did she have for dinner?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, I meant that she was just having breakfast.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh she just finished her breakfast, and what did she have for breakfast?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bacon and pancakes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, pancakes and bacon, with her friend having breakfast with her. Who was her friend that had breakfast with her?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mischief.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Mischief Mouse was over for breakfast and they both had pancakes and bacon and they finished eating and Hattie Mouse said, "Excuse me, I had a little burp." And Mistress Mouse said, "Hehe, you're excused."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Not Mistress Mouse, Mischief Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I'm sorry, Mischief Mouse. And Mischief Mouse said, "I would like to go on an adventure. What would you like to do on our adventure, Hattie?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, it's raining outside.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hattie said, "No, it's raining outside." But Mischief said, "We could still go on an adventure. Did you know?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: If you just get your galoshes and your raincoat and your hoodie...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so they both got dressed...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: ...and an umbrella.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They both got dressed in all their rain gear and they got ready to go out and their umbrellas and their jackets and their galoshes. And then they opened the door and then the first thing they saw was...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A big puddle and what did they do?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A big puddle! And what did they do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Splash over it!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They splashed right over it! But then there was some mud, so what did they do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They splashed over it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They splashed right through the mud. Squish, squish, squish. And it made some yucky mucky mud sounds. Squish splash. And then poking up out of the mud, who did they see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nobody.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Nobody. Except for one little wriggly wiggly worm and the worm said "Hello, my name is..." what's the worm's name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wesley.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "My name is Wesley the worm. What are you doing?" And what did Hattie say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Walking.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Walking. Would you like to come with us?" And Wesley worm said "Okay, I'm a little slow, but I'll come with you." And so Wesley and Hattie and Mischief, they went on a walk and they went down the block through the rain and then they got to... where did they get to?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A construction site.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then they hopped over it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They hopped over it but there was a big digger and the digger said, "Hello my name is..." what was the digger's name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Whopper.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Whopper? My name is Whopper and I am a digger and I like to dig. Watch this! And the digger stretched out its arm and plunged its bucket into the pile of dirt and went scoop and picked up a big pile of dirt and he said, "What should I do with my scoop of dirt everyone?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Put on the road.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Put it on the road, okay. And Whopper the digger dumped his dirt right on the road.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then smooth it down.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then smooth it down. And Whopper the digger smoothed the dirt all over the road to make it nice and smooth for the cars. And what did Hattie and Mischief and Wesley say?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They said, "Wow, that's a beautiful smooth road, digger."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now you can go walking some more.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then they went walking some more and then what did they find next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nothing else.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Nothing else. Well they decided to go to a store to shop for something. What were they going to shop for?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A purple purse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A purple purse. Who was the purple purse going to be for? Was it going to be a present for someone?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah it was going to be a present for Mistress.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ah, a present for Mistress. Is it Mistress's birthday?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh my gosh, Mistress Mouse's birthday is coming up and Hattie and Mischief and Wesley the Worm...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And everyone's going to come and sing Happy Birthday.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, everyone's gonna come sing Happy Birthday to Mistress Mouse. And so what store did they go to to buy the purse?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The purse store.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The purse store. And it was on Cornell?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Cornell Street.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh it was on Cornell Street which Hattie lives on.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Right.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hattie lives on Cornell Street of course. And so they went to the purse store and they picked out a perfect purse. What color was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Orange.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: An orange purse.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: With sparkles on it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And they decided we're going to bring that to the birthday party and give it to Mistress Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, it's now Mistress Mouse's birthday.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the next day it was Mistress Mouse's birthday and everyone came to the party. And what did they do at the party?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They sang Happy Birthday and ate some cake.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Some delicious cake. What flavor was the cake?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Chocolate.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Chocolate cake. And did they have candles?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And how old are you, Mistress Mouse?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Three.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mistress Mouse, you're three years old. You're getting so big. And okay time to do presents. And Hattie the mouse brought out a big shiny wrapped box with a big bow on it and said, "Here's your present, it's from me and Mischief and Wesley worm." And Mistress Mouse unwrapped the box and pulled off the bow and popped the box open and looked inside and she said, "Wow! I've always wanted an orange sparkle purse. Thank you, it's beautiful." And that was the end of Mistress Mouse's birthday party. And what did everyone else do next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I think I'm done with the stars.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh and that was the end of stars and stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I can make snow fingers.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, oh you're making shadows with your fingers. That's cool. Wow, look at that. I love how you're doing that. Here, can I show you something?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: If you make a shape like this, it looks like a mouse. Do you want to try it? Here, I'll hold the light up. Now if you face the wall and make a shape like this. Argh argh argh. Do you want to try?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I can make a face like this.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow. Hello, I see you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hello, I see you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, that's a nice shadow puppet you're making. Oh, now I have bunny ears. Oh, I'm a bunny. Hop, hop, hop. Oh, you're blocking your shadow. Okay.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Mischief Mouse
characters.description: Reigning from a purple-striped Jovian castle, this affable mouse King—a playful-named companion to Pinecone Mouse—is a seasoned adventurer, guardian of the Underground Library, and Hattie’s steadfast ally. Between savoring artisanal cheeses and awaiting a rugged cardboard replacement for his fragile Lego hat, he spends leisure hours meticulously constructing and gleefully smashing soaring towers. A pancake-loving monarch who once tended shop for mountain-dwelling traffic cones, his cosmic quests recently shifted from the stars to the speedway; having borrowed a tow truck from Mistress Mouse, he proved a formidable competitor who nearly clinched a stunning victory. Whether piloting starships or joining the group’s latest trek to Nana’s house, he remains a vigilant protector and devoted friend across the galaxy.

characters.name: Mistress Mouse
characters.description: A polite three-year-old mouse and resourceful shopkeeper, this community cornerstone balances a bold artistic spirit with sharp mechanical grit. Whether crafting intricate Lego creations in her sunroom or piloting her signature pink tow truck to solve puzzles and assist neighbors, she is a master of the road and a multi-talented creator. From famously adorning cars with blue stripes to dominating the race track, her inventive soul is always on the move. Usually seen in a mushroom-adorned cardboard hat with an orange sparkle purse, this resourceful driver remains an essential, evocative fixture of every neighborhood adventure.

characters.name: Wesley the Worm
characters.description: A slow but friendly wriggly worm who joins the group on their rainy walk.

characters.name: Whopper
characters.description: A large construction digger who likes to dig and smooth out the road for others.

places.name: Hattie's House
places.description: Nestled in a sun-drenched tree across from the town park, Hattie’s arboreal sanctuary on Mouse Street doubles as a vibrant Cornell Street workshop for designing hats and a peaceful retreat for resting after long days of painting. This cozy home, cluttered with the tools of her trade, is a place of shared morning breakfasts with Mischief and savory meals cooked alongside her Nana. Whether lulled by the rhythmic drumming of rainfall or the quiet exhaustion of her artistry, Hattie finds her solace here, drifting into restorative naps amidst the creative clutter.

places.name: Construction Site
places.description: Just down the block, a skeletal labyrinth of towering cranes and idling bulldozers serves as a high-octane arena where mice compete in blurring, high-speed races. This mechanical sanctuary is the domain of Whopper the digger, where the air hums with the scent of grease and the frantic adrenaline of the tiny circuit.

places.name: The Purse Store
places.description: A shop located on Cornell Street where the characters buy a birthday present.

places.name: Cornell Street
places.description: Bisected by a stark yellow line, this celestial artery cuts through a dense forest to chart a path toward the Mookers' planet. A cosmic thoroughfare where the infinite is stitched into the earthly, it anchors local commerce—typified by a towering tree of honey—alongside the residence of Uncle X, the house of the surreal bedroom feast, and Hattie’s home. It remains the vital threshold where the mundane pulse of the woods meets the stars, marking the point where Hattie begins her journey to find medical help.
